title: descurainia incisa var. viscosa
intro: Descurainia incisa var. viscosa, also known as sticky false wallflower or sticky tansy mustard, is a plant species in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its sticky leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Descurainia incisa var. viscosa include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used fresh or dried for medicinal purposes.
